answer:
\boxed{D}

The most effective way to avoid failure due to poor clasp design is to **clasp only teeth with adequate crown height and stable bone support**, as these provide better retention and support for partial dentures. While stress breakers (A) and bar clasps (B) can help, they are not universally applicable or as foundational as selecting appropriate teeth. Altering tooth contours (C) may compromise natural tooth structure unnecessarily.
